description_long: This object is a rustic wooden fence composed of vertical pickets connected by horizontal rails, forming two main sections with a central gate. The pickets are evenly spaced, with the tops cut into a simple pointed design, giving the fence a traditional, utilitarian appearance. The central gate is distinguishable by its slightly different structure and the presence of hinges, allowing for entry and exit. The wood appears weathered, with a natural brown-gray coloration, suggesting outdoor use and exposure to the elements. The fence is relatively low in height, suitable for marking boundaries in residential or park settings without obstructing views. There are no ornate or decorative elements, emphasizing function over form.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden fence with vertical pickets, horizontal rails, and a central gate, designed for boundary marking in outdoor urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing two main fence sections with a central gate and evenly spaced vertical pickets.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow depth of the fence and the alignment of the pickets and rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the gate and the structure of the horizontal rails supporting the pickets.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the thin profile and the arrangement of the fence sections.
